title: Thunderbird Android , 0Auth2 and POP3
creator: Luddite
content:
Hi,

I'm looking to make Gmail my principal email account, instead of using my ISP account. Gmail requires 0Auth2 authentication, as many will know.

I'm a Luddite and prefer to use POP3, for a number of reasons. Thunderbird works fine (with POP3 Gmail), on my computer. Unfortunately, 0Auth2 doesn't appear as an authentication option, for POP3, in Tbird for Android. So the TB android app forces IMAP on my tablet and phone.

Is there any way around this? Are there any plans to incorporate 0Auth2 authentication for POP3 within Tbird for Android?

Thanks

title: Only see old mails in my Infomaniak Inbox
creator: rdscorreia74
content:
Hey there. Hope you're all doing just fine.

TL;DR Tried moving all my email messages from GMail to Infomaniak. Worked fine because I can see all my old GMail messages now on my Infomaniak Inbox using Thunderbird on my PC. But my phone refuses to show me the latest messages. It only shows me messages from 2025. --- Now with a lot more detail. I use Thunderbird as my email client of choice on both my Android phone and on my Linux (Manjaro) PC. TB on the PC is running 140.8.1esr 64 bit flatpak installed. TB on my phone reads 16.1 and has been installed from the github repository using Obtainium. I use IMAP for receiving and SMTP for sending. No POP3.

I have two email accounts. One at GMail and another at Infomaniak. Up until recently, I've only had the GMail account set up on both my phone and PC. I've been using Infomaniak as webmail just for testing. But recently I've decided to add Infomaniak's to both my Thunderbirds and move all my emails from GMail to Infomaniak. I used Google Takeout to download the mbox files (inbox, archive and sent), then using my PC I loaded the mbox files in my "Local Files" and finally I moved the messages to my Infomaniak correct IMAP folders.

It all looks just fine in my Linux PC. But on my phone I can only see messages from 2025 on my inbox. And I have many messages from 2026. I can see them using TB on my PC, or via the webmail. They just won't show on my phone.

EDIT: It only shows me messages from 2025 and prior to that. But it doesn't show me all messages from 2025. Many messages from 2025 are also missing. And from previous years too. This is not happening on the inbox folder only. It is also happening on all the other folders too. It's as if TB was filtering the messages. It's driving me nuts.

I've already tried clearing TB's data and import my settings. Still only have messages from 2025. What could be going on with Tbird on my phone?? Thanks in advance. Cheers

title: Client cannot see any IMAP folders other than .INBOX
creator: mcs_firefox
content:
I'm running Thunderbird at the desktop on various Linux and Windows boxes. These instances, along with other interfaces like RoundCube and the like can use my IMAP server to access all the folders in any given email account, move mails between them, add/remove/edit folders etc.

Thunderbird for Android can't even see them, not even the standard sent/trash/etc. options - only .INBOX. I have all the folders in my subscriptions file but nothing shows when I query the folder list from the client ("Manage Folders") and in any case I have unchecked "only list subscribed folders" in the settings for my account.

Any idea what I need to do to get at folders in this instance, because this setup is rendering the client semi-useless for me?

title: Inbox list of emails not being refreshed for first/top message in list
creator: timothy.p.schultz
content:
The Thunderbird for Android app updated yesterday to version 17.0. Ever since the update, I've noticed an undesired behavior (bug?). Whether I have one new email or have several new/unread emails, whenever I read the top/latest email and then delete it, when returned to the Inbox list of emails, the message I just read and deleted will show that it is still there and unread. However, if I simply touch the screen and drag the list of messages upward or downward slightly, then the email is deleted (removed from the Inbox).

The same behavior happens when simply reading the first (top of the list) message and return to the Inbox... the message will show unread, but if I drag the list up slightly, it will then show the email as having been read.

This behavior does not exist for other emails in the list, only the first one (top of the list/latest to come in).

Perhaps I'm not the first to report this new and undesired behavior. Is there some setting that I'm not aware of that I can set to correct it, or does it need to be fixed in a future update? I tried rebooting the phone last night, which made no difference to this behavior.

I'm running a Samsung Galaxy S22 running Android 16.

title: what benefits can I expect from consulting experienced braces specialists in Dubai?"
creator: whitetick1983
content:
Braces specialists in Dubai are highly trained dental professionals who focus on diagnosing, preventing, and correcting misaligned teeth and jaw issues. Unlike general dentists, these specialists undergo additional years of education and clinical training to provide expert orthodontic care. Choosing braces specialists ensures a precise treatment plan tailored to each patient’s needs, whether for children, teenagers, or adults seeking a straighter smile. Dubai offers advanced dental technology, modern facilities, and highly skilled professionals, making it an ideal destination for orthodontic care.

Who Needs Braces?

Braces specialists treat a variety of dental issues including crooked or crowded teeth, gaps between teeth, overbite, underbite, crossbite, jaw misalignment, and difficulty chewing or speaking. Both children and adults can benefit from braces, as modern orthodontic techniques are designed to be effective, comfortable, and discreet. Early consultation allows specialists to address potential alignment problems, preventing complications later in life, while adults can achieve a straighter smile without compromising aesthetics or lifestyle.

Types of Braces Offered by Specialists

Braces specialists in Dubai provide multiple treatment options to suit individual needs and preferences. Traditional metal braces are highly effective for complex alignment issues and remain a popular choice for patients of all ages. Ceramic braces offer a more aesthetic alternative using tooth-colored or clear brackets, making them less noticeable while still providing reliable results. Lingual braces are attached behind the teeth, providing an invisible treatment option for those seeking discretion. Self-ligating braces use specialized clips to hold wires, reducing friction and improving comfort, sometimes even shortening treatment duration. Clear aligners are a removable and nearly invisible option, ideal for adults or professionals seeking a flexible and discreet solution for teeth straightening. Each type of braces is designed to address specific orthodontic concerns, and specialists help patients choose the most suitable option based on their oral health, lifestyle, and aesthetic goals.

Benefits of Consulting Braces Specialists

Working with braces specialists in Dubai provides numerous advantages. Properly aligned teeth are easier to clean, reducing the risk of cavities, gum disease, and plaque buildup, which improves long-term oral health. Braces also enhance facial aesthetics, boosting confidence in both social and professional interactions. Correcting bite issues improves chewing efficiency, reduces jaw strain, and prevents excessive wear on teeth. Additionally, orthodontic treatment guided by specialists provides predictable and long-lasting results, ensuring that patients enjoy the benefits of a healthy and well-aligned smile for years to come.

The Treatment Process

The treatment journey begins with an initial consultation, where the specialist assesses teeth, gums, and jaw alignment to determine the most suitable treatment plan. Advanced diagnostic tools such as digital scans, X-rays, and photographs allow precise planning and sometimes provide simulations of the expected outcome. Once the braces or aligner trays are ready, they are carefully applied or fitted to begin the alignment process. Patients return periodically for adjustments to ensure teeth move safely and effectively toward their desired positions. After braces are removed, retainers are prescribed to maintain the alignment and prevent relapse. Regular follow-ups and consistent care are essential to achieving and preserving optimal results.

Lifestyle Tips During Treatment

During orthodontic treatment, patients are advised to maintain proper oral hygiene, including brushing after meals, flossing with specialized tools, and using mouthwash to minimize bacterial buildup. Avoiding hard, sticky, or sugary foods prevents damage to braces and supports treatment progress. Following the specialist’s instructions for care, attending scheduled appointments, and using recommended orthodontic accessories, such as wax or floss threaders, contributes to a smooth and effective treatment experience.

Choosing the Right Braces Specialist

Selecting a qualified braces specialist is crucial for successful treatment. Factors to consider include advanced orthodontic training, experience with various types of braces, access to modern diagnostic and treatment technology, and a patient-centered approach to care. Checking reviews and patient feedback provides insights into the quality of treatment and the overall experience. A specialist who communicates clearly and designs a personalized treatment plan ensures that patients feel confident and informed throughout the orthodontic journey.

Maintaining Results After Treatment

The retention phase following braces removal is essential to preserve the new alignment. Wearing retainers as advised by the specialist prevents teeth from shifting back. Maintaining good oral hygiene, attending follow-up visits, and avoiding habits such as teeth grinding or nail-biting are critical to ensuring long-term success. Proper care during this phase ensures that patients continue to enjoy a straight, healthy, and confident smile.

Consulting braces specialists in Dubai is a reliable step toward achieving a straight, healthy, and attractive smile. With access to advanced orthodontic technology, personalized care, and highly skilled professionals, patients can expect effective treatment with long-lasting results. Braces not only improve appearance but also enhance oral functionality, reduce the risk of future dental problems, and provide a confident, beautiful smile. Choosing the right specialist and following proper care guidelines ensures a successful orthodontic journey and lasting oral health benefits.
